en.extract:
The black sittella (Daphoenositta miranda) is one of two species of birds in the family Neosittidae.
It is found in endemic to New Guinea, where it is found in the highlands.


References

del Hoyo, J.; Elliot, A. & Christie D. (editors). (2007). Handbook of the Birds of the World. Volume 12: Picathartes to Tits and Chickadees. Lynx Edicions. ISBN 978-84-96553-42-2

[[File:Daphoenositta miranda - Nigel Voaden - 3114203.jpeg|thumb|Live bird]]
The '''black sittella''' ('''''Daphoenositta miranda''''') is one of two species of [[bird]]s in the family [[Neosittidae]].
It is found in endemic to [[New Guinea]], where it is found in the highlands.
